jobs.title: Principal Product Manager, AI & Mobile Strategy
jobs.jd_full:
At Constant Contact, we are seriously awesome people who take ownership and make an impact by operating with the mindset, integrity and courage of a small business owner. There’s something so profoundly rewarding about knowing that your work is empowering people everywhere to pursue their dreams. Here, we all play an integral part in helping business owners, entrepreneurs, non-profits and individuals to succeed by giving them all the help and tools they need to grow online. We’re energized by new challenges and new possibilities-and we’re just getting started!

We are also actively pushing what is possible with AI when it comes to how we work and how we create value for our customers. We see this year as a pivotal moment for how AI will change the product development lifecycle, and we’re doubling down on it in all departments.

We are a hybrid workforce, combining the convenience of remote work with access to our modern office locations for in-person collaboration, training, and socialization. Here, you’ll find a supportive culture that values inclusivity, risk-taking, and innovation.

About the Role

We have an opening for a Principal Product Manager to help us lead the charge in making Constant Contact an AI-native, mobile-first marketing platform.

This is a high-leverage "builder" role designed for someone who thrives in ambiguity. Whether you are launching a brand-new AI-driven product line or modernizing an existing platform pillar, you will be on the cutting edge of how the product development lifecycle is changing. You will form clear hypotheses, use AI to test and refine them, and work closely with Design and Engineering to bring ideas to market. The focus is on rapid experimentation and learning as quickly as possible.

What You’ll Do

  • Drive Growth Metrics: Own the strategy and execution for key growth levers, specifically focusing on customer outcomes.
  • Prove Customer Value: Develop and defend "strong opinions" on how we demonstrate value to users. You’ll use insightful research and behavioral metrics to build features that make the benefits of Constant Contact impossible to ignore.
  • Lead through Influence: As a Principal IC, you will lead a group of passionate engineers and designers to deliver a vision that is actionable, research-backed, and ROI-driven.
  • Mentor the Craft: Act as a "multiplier" for the product organization. You will mentor other PMs, leveling up their product discovery, strategic thinking, and execution skills to help the entire team move faster than ever before.
  • Collaborate & Navigate: Partner with the rest of the organization to clarify requirements and navigate highly complex, ambiguous problem spaces across our squads.

Who You Are

You are a seasoned product leader with a track record of "doing it before." You understand the nuances of SaaS growth and are passionate about the intersection of design excellence and business results.

  • Experienced Leader: 8+ years in Product Management, specifically within SaaS. You have a proven track record of moving the needle on retention and growth metrics.
  • Strategic & Opinionated: You don’t just take orders; you bring a perspective on what "good" looks like. You can articulate and defend a product vision to all audiences, including the executive team.

AI Enabled & Exploring: You know that the future of product management and PDLC go through amplifying your efforts through AI. You are actively trialling and experimenting with tools that help you become a 10x product leader.

  • Mentor & Coach: You have a natural ability to level up those around you. You value teamwork and can give/solicit the kind of feedback that raises the bar for the entire organization.
  • User Champion: You can empathize deeply with small business owners and champion their cause throughout the design and development process.
  • Agile & Adaptable: You thrive in fast-paced, collaborative environments and can balance the need for great design with the urgency of short deadlines.
  • Education: Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Computer Science, or a related field.

Nice to Have

  • Experience in the SMB (Small-to-Medium Business) space.
  • Background in early-stage startups or "internal startups" within larger companies.
  • Experience mentoring senior PMs and setting organizational product standards.

The specific salary offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors including the candidate’s experience, their education and work location. In addition, some roles may be eligible for additional on target commission pay or bonus. The compensation package includes health and welfare benefits including paid leave.

Pay Transparency - All Full Time Employees
$159,500—$191,000 USD

Why You’ll Love Us:

  • We celebrate one another’s differences. We are proud of our culture of diversity and inclusion, and we're always working hard to strengthen and improve this culture. We have programs in place that bring us together on important issues and provide educational opportunities for all employees.
  • Join the experts. If you're passionate about supporting businesses and organizations, you won’t find a better spot to share your talent. We’re dedicated to improving the quality of our product from a customer standpoint and have a true desire to enable the success of businesses, non-profit organizations, and community groups.
  • You’ll have opportunities to grow your career. We encourage and support our team members to learn different aspects of the business, take on stretch assignments, seek coaching opportunities and professional development opportunities.
  • A generous paid time off policy and a competitive benefits package that supports the health and well-being of you and your family

At Constant Contact, we’re all about work flexibility and are proud to serve our customers as a hybrid workforce. Our hybrid work model combines the convenience of remote work with access to our great office locations to collaborate in-person, participate in live trainings, and socialize with colleagues.

Application Deadline: 30 days. We may fill this job opening prior to the deadline if a candidate is selected by us.

jobs.title: Data Science Product Manager, Yield Optimization
jobs.jd_full:
About Trellis

Trellis is rewriting the insurance experience from the inside out. We’re the tech company behind Savvy, our licensed insurance agency, and we’re bringing clarity and ease to a space known for… the opposite. With powerful tools, clean design, and a customer-first mindset, we’re making insurance shopping refreshingly effortless.

We’re a profitable, fast-growing Series A startup backed by General Catalyst, QED, NYCA, and Amex Ventures. As a remote-first team, we move quickly, experiment boldly, and build with intention.

If you’re craving meaningful impact, energized by ambiguity, and are ready to build alongside exceptional teammates, you’re going to love doing your best work at Trellis.

Your Mission

We are seeking a Data Science Product Manager, Yield Optimization to lead the development, implementation, and ongoing improvement of predictive models that are central to our marketplace and revenue generation by ensuring that we show the optimal offers to our users. This critical role is focused on continuously improving decision-making for every individual user—from the moment they arrive to their final placement with an insurance provider—directly impacting funnel optimization and our revenue engine.

This is a highly cross-functional role where you will collaborate closely with internal Engineering and business stakeholders to turn messy, real-world data into clear insights and working models, while also interfacing externally with our ad and call buyers. Success requires a blend of product ownership, business acumen, and full-stack data science proficiency to define problems, prioritize opportunities, and ensure the models you own drive maximum business impact.

This is a fully remote position, based in the US or Canada, and will report directly into Keith Schreer, VP of Data.

We believe in paying competitively for great work. As a remote team, we set our salaries at the 75th percentile of national market data in our industry. Candidates based in SF or NYC will be benchmarked at the 90th percentile. You can count on your pay being in line with (or above) the market. For this role, the base salary range for the 75th percentile is $195,000 – $205,000. We review compensation twice a year to make sure we’re staying competitive and rewarding our team’s contributions.

What You'll Do:

  1. Lead Product & Planning: Partner with Engineering and Business teams to seamlessly integrate models into our recommendation engine.

  2. Own Predictive Model Roadmap: Drive the full lifecycle of our yield optimization models, from ideation and development to production monitoring and iteration.

  3. Drive Revenue: Continuously evaluate and improve model accuracy and interpretability to directly impact key business KPIs like revenue and conversion rates.

  4. Cultivate a Thriving Marketplace: Use sophisticated models to intelligently match users to the best offers, driving value for our internal agency and external buyers.

  5. Drive External Partnership Success: Interface directly with external partners, ensuring mutual success and aligning model performance with partner goals.

What You'll Need to Succeed:

  • 7+ years of experience in Data Science roles implementing predictive models.

  • Proven Full-Stack Data Proficiency: end-to-end ownership of predictive models used in production.

  • Experience with recommendation systems, ad tech, or yield optimization models.

  • Expert proficiency in SQL and the ability to independently source, wrangle, and understand data across the full stack.

  • Business & Product Acumen: Strong ability to translate complex business problems (like optimizing revenue) into clear analytical questions and communicate insights/tradeoffs to non-technical stakeholders and external partners.

  • High Autonomy & Execution: Demonstrated capacity to operate with high autonomy, follow through on unowned problems, and thrive in a fast-paced, scrappy startup environment.

  • Communication: Clear, concise, and professional written and verbal communication for both internal alignment and external partner management.

  • Preferred: Experience with ML platforms (e.g., DataRobot) or a background in Ad Tech/Insurtech is a plus.

Why Trellis? Because you deserve a career that’s exciting, meaningful, and surrounded by people who lift you up.

We’re a group of curious, mission-driven humans rewriting the insurance experience and having a lot of fun along the way. At Trellis, you’ll make a real impact on a product millions rely on, and grow alongside a company that’s scaling fast.

What sets Trellis apart:

✨ A transparent, collaborative culture where ideas win, not titles
🚀 Big opportunities to take ownership and chart your growth
💰 Competitive compensation (75th+ percentile)

🏡 Fully remote across the US & Canada

🎉 Quarterly virtual and/or in-person events that keep us connected

Plus, the benefits are built to support your whole life:

🌴 Flexible vacation (yes, actually flexible)
🩺 100% employer-paid health insurance for employees
💻 Home office budget to create your perfect setup
🧘 Wellness programming, because balance matters
💸 401(k) and HSA contributions, FSAs, bonuses & equity opportunities

🐣 Paid parental leave

👉 If you want a role where you’ll grow, be trusted, and build something that genuinely improves people’s lives, Trellis is the place. Join us.

We are an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Note: all employees must complete a background check prior to starting employment with Trellis or its subsidiaries

Compensation Range: $195K - $205K

Through strong technical acumen and a user‑centric mindset, you translate strategy into actionable requirements and shepherd product initiatives from concept through commercial launch.

What We Offer

  • Competitive compensation

  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage

  • Flexible and paid time off

  • Remote and hybrid work options

  • Tuition reimbursement

  • Employee assistance and wellness programs

  • Life and disability insurance

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Partner with Product Line Leaders and Upstream Product Managers to ensure alignment with overall portfolio strategy and long‑term product vision.

  • Stay current on emerging cardiac safety technologies, industry standards, regulatory expectations, and competitive landscape.

  • Build and maintain a multi‑year roadmap that encompasses releases, initiatives, and epics tied to overall launch plans.

  • Collaborate closely with engineering teams to define architecture, user experience, and innovative capabilities across Cardiac Safety components.

  • Lead business architecture for assigned components, including user flows and data workflows, ensuring cohesion with the broader cardiac safety platform architecture.

  • Validate concepts, themes, and MVP definitions through research and ongoing interaction with internal and external users.

  • Balance technical debt, architectural considerations, and new feature development to ensure sustainable product growth.

  • Transfer business architecture and use‑case knowledge to Product Owners and cross‑functional teams.

  • Act as a subject matter expert to support internal teams, customer engagements, and cross‑functional education.

  • Contribute to the development of documentation, user materials, and internal knowledge bases.

  • Lead cross‑functional release activities and support commercial launch readiness.

  • May manage individual contributors and lead through influence across global teams.

What We Look For

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, science, technology, or medicine; MBA preferred.

  • 3+ years of experience in product management.

  • Experience in healthcare and/or life sciences.

  • Full life‑cycle product experience within healthcare or clinical trials.

  • Strong technical acumen with the ability to translate business needs for technical teams.

  • Experience collaborating with UX/UI teams to develop user and data workflows.

  • Demonstrated ability to work directly and effectively with customers.

  • Proven prioritization skills and ability to align activities with strategic objectives.

  • Experience leading across diverse stakeholder groups through influence.

  • Ability to make data‑driven decisions in fast‑paced, agile environments.

  • Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills in English.

  • Ability to travel 25%+.

jobs.title: Collections Strategy & Operations Manager, MX
jobs.jd_full:
Join the Tilt team

At Tilt, we see a side of people that traditional lenders miss. Our mobile-first products and machine learning-powered credit models look beyond credit scores, using over 250 real-time financial signals to recognize real potential. With millions of customers worldwide, we're not just changing how people access financial products — we're creating a new credit system that backs the working, whatever they're working toward.

México was Tilt’s first market outside the US. Launched in 2022, we’ve already helped thousands of customers access fair credit in moments of need — using alternative data and technology to serve those often overlooked by traditional lenders. We’re scaling rapidly and preparing to launch new products that will expand our reach and deepen our impact across the country.

The Opportunity: Collections Strategy and Ops Manager

We are seeking a Collections Strategy and Ops Manager to build a world-class collections function. This is a rare opportunity to take full ownership of collections strategy, risk segmentation, outreach channels, and analytics in a fast-growing, high- impact environment. If you’re looking for a foundational role where you can have massive impact scaling operations that expand financial inclusion, and a wide scope within a high performing team, this is the role for you.

The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with deep expertise in collections, risk, and analytics. They will have the opportunity to shape the vision, design best-in-class collections strategies, and eventually grow a high-performing team. This individual should be excited about getting in the weeds and hands-on leading analyses. They will drive experimentation, optimize collections performance, and implement data-driven decision-making across all aspects of the function.

This is a remote role with a preference for individuals based in México or in a similar time zone. Occasional travel will be required for company onsites and market visits (minimum 4-6 times per year).

How You'll Make an Impact

  • Test and iterate rapidly on different collections strategies, testing new outreach techniques, incentives, and risk-based approaches.

  • Design and implement predictive collections scoring and automation, leveraging segmentation, data analytics, machine learning, and vendors to optimize recovery rates.

  • Develop and scale collections outreach models—across digital, automated, and agent-led channels—to maximize efficiency and performance.

  • Coordinate and manage relationships with external collection agencies to ensure aligned strategy, consistent performance, and compliance.

  • Build and lead a high-performing collections strategy and analytics team, ensuring best-in-class execution and continuous improvement.

  • Work cross-functionally with Credit, Data Science, Product, Growth, and Operations teams to refine collections strategy, enhance underwriting insights, and drive innovation.

  • Own key collections KPIs, including delinquency rates, recovery efficiency, and overall portfolio performance, ensuring financial targets are met.

  • Ensure compliance with all relevant regulatory requirements

Why You're a Great Fit

  • 4+ years of experience in collections strategy, risk analytics, and credit risk management in financial services, preferably in consumer lending fintech.

  • Proven expertise in data-driven collections strategies, risk segmentation, and model-driven outreach optimization.

  • Deep familiarity with AI-driven collections scoring, automation, and optimization techniques is highly preferred.

  • Strong analytical skills; Happy to be hands-on and in the weeds driving analysis themselves.

  • Experience working with cross-functional teams and managing complex initiatives in high-growth, fast-scaling environments.

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, ability to communicate to an executive audience.

  • Proficiency in SQL and analytics platforms is a strong plus.

  • Experience working with regulatory frameworks and compliance requirements in lending/collections is a plus.

Don’t meet every qualification? We care about potential over your past. If you're bringing ambition and drive to what we're building, we want to hear from you.

What you'll get at Tilt

  • Virtual-first teamwork: The Tilt team is collaborating across 14 countries, 12 time zones, and counting. You’ll get started with a WFH office reimbursement.

  • Competitive pay: We're big on potential, and it's reflected in our competitive compensation packages and generous equity.

  • Complete support: Find flexible health plans at every premium level, and substantial subsidies that stand up to global standards.

  • Visibility is yours: You can count on direct exposure to our leadership team — we’re a team where good ideas travel quickly.

  • Paid global onsites: Magic happens IRL: we gather twice yearly to reconnect over shared meals or kayaking adventures. (We’ve visited Vail, San Diego, and Mexico City, to name a few.)

  • Impact is recognized: Growth opportunities follow your contributions, not rigid promotion timelines.

The Tilt Way

We're looking for people who chase excellence and impact. Those who stand behind their work, celebrating the wins and learning from the missteps equally. We foster an environment where every voice is valued and mutual respect is non-negotiable — brilliant jerks need not apply. We're in this together, working to expand access to fair credit and prove that people are incredible. When you join us, it's not just another day at the [virtual] office, you're helping millions of hardworking people reach better financial futures.

You’re pushing ahead in your career? We can get behind that. Join us in building the credit system that people deserve.

jobs.title: Lead Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
ABOUT TIDE

At Tide we help SMEs save time (and money) in the running of their businesses by not only offering business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of highly usable and connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.

Tide is transforming the small business banking market with over 1.8 million members globally across the UK, India, Germany and France.

Using advanced technology, all solutions are designed with SMEs in mind. With quick onboarding, low fees and innovative features, we thrive on making data-driven decisions to serve our mission: to help SMEs save both time (and money) so they can get back to doing what they love.

Tide facts:

  • Tide is available for UK, Indian, German and French SMEs
  • Over 1.8 million members: 800,000 UK and 1,000,000 in India and growing rapidly
  • Over $300 million raised in funding
  • Over 2,500 Tideans globally - we’re diversity champions!
  • We have offices in Central London, with a member support and technology centre in Sofia, Bulgaria, technology centres in Serbia, Romania, Lithuania and Hyderabad and offices in Gurugram and New Delhi, and in Berlin, Paris and Luxembourg.

ABOUT TIDE:

At Tide, we are building a finance platform designed to save small businesses time and money. We provide our members with business accounts and related banking services, but also a comprehensive set of connected administrative solutions from invoicing to accounting.

Launched in 2017, Tide is now used by over 1 million small businesses across the world and is available to UK, Indian and German SMEs. Headquartered in central London, with offices in Sofia, Hyderabad, Delhi, Berlin and Belgrade, Tide employs over 2,000 employees.

Tide is rapidly growing, expanding into new markets and always looking for passionate and driven people. Join us in our mission to empower small businesses and help them save time and money.

ABOUT THE TEAM:

You’ll be joining a global team of Product Managers within our Partner Credit Services (PCS) business area, a group on a mission to help our members get the right credit, enabling them to trade, plan, and grow their business with confidence. The PCS team simplifies access to credit by building seamless, transparent, and scalable lending experiences in partnership with leading lenders.

ABOUT THE CHALLENGE:

The first 30 days of a member’s experience are critical to long-term engagement. In this role, your key challenge will be to define what the ideal post-onboarding experience looks like for new members, across different business types.

Your vertical will be responsible for:

  • Identifying and segmenting businesses based on their context and needs.
  • Determining the next best product (NBP) for each segment.
  • Designing journeys that maximize adoption, retention, and LTV.
  • Collaborating with data, marketing, product, and engineering to create and scale smart, automated onboarding experiences.
  • Validating hypotheses and continuously optimizing based on behavioural insights.

This is a unique opportunity to own a greenfield domain and shape a product experience that has massive revenue and engagement upside.

ABOUT THE ROLE:

As a Lead Product Manager, you’ll shape the strategy and vision for our credit products, driving innovation and scale across markets. This is a high-ownership role that blends strategic thinking, execution excellence, and cross-functional leadership. You’ll work closely with teams across Product, Engineering, Design, Data, Risk, and Partnerships to create a world-class credit experience that meets the evolving needs of business owners globally.

As a Lead Product Manager you will:

  • Own the vision, strategy, and roadmap for key product domains within Credit, driving innovation and global scale in collaboration with the Head of Product and leadership team.
  • Lead with deep ownership,  from discovery and design through delivery and iteration,  ensuring your products deliver tangible user value and measurable business outcomes.
  • Drive new product development across both customer-facing and internal initiatives, from greenfield concepts to optimisation of existing solutions.
  • Design globally scalable solutions, balancing market nuances and regulatory complexity across Tide’s key regions
  • Collaborate with platform teams to elevate the foundational experience, ensuring consistency, scalability, and alignment with organisational OKRs.
  • Champion cross-functional excellence by partnering with Engineering, Design, Data, Product Marketing, and Partnerships to ship impactful, high-quality experiences.
  • Lead discovery and experimentation, combining qualitative insights and quantitative data to identify opportunities, test hypotheses, and shape the product direction.
  • Communicate with influence; clearly articulating strategy, priorities, and progress to senior stakeholders, inspiring alignment and confidence across the organisation.
  • Be the voice of the customer, ensuring member needs remain central to every decision while advocating for simplicity, transparency, and trust in credit experiences.
  • Mentor and empower Product Managers within the team, helping shape their growth as the Credit group scales globally.
  • Champion product excellence and agility, continuously improving ways of working, decision velocity, and collaboration across teams.

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

This role is for you if you’re a seasoned product professional with a deep understanding of user-first product development, a hypothesis- and data-driven mindset, and a passion for iterative discovery and delivery.

  • You have 10+ years of experience in product management, with hands-on involvement throughout the full product development lifecycle, particularly in iterative product discovery and delivery.
  • You are both strategic and execution-focused, with a proven ability to drive product improvements and deliver measurable results.
  • You enjoy hands-on product management and are excited to contribute to a team that’s scaling, bringing your expertise to build great products.
  • You naturally spot opportunities for improvement in processes, tools, or team workflows and can drive impactful changes with agility.
  • You’re comfortable navigating ambiguity and can break down greenfield areas into clear steps, milestones, and actionable plans while remaining flexible to adapt as needed.
  • You are an excellent communicator, able to tailor your messaging to resonate across all levels, including senior leadership, and you are a compelling storyteller in both written and spoken English.
  • You are outcome-driven, focused on creating value for both users and the business, and can confidently pivot strategies to maintain alignment with goals.
  • You are passionate about delivering exceptional user experiences, paying close attention to UX details and ensuring end-to-end solutions meet diverse consumer needs.
  • You can define and track key metrics, combining qualitative and quantitative data to guide decisions and prioritize effectively.
  • You excel in product discovery, confidently driving validation and iteration within both the problem and solution space.
  • You are adept at simplifying complexity, finding innovative solutions to challenging problems and cutting through ambiguity with ease.
  • Ideally, you have experience working on platform or foundational products, and you understand how to scale products across teams and organizations.
  • Bonus: You have experience in startups, scale-ups, global products, or FinTech.
  • Bonus: You bring technical experience or education that enables you to collaborate effectively with engineering teams.

WHAT YOU’LL GET IN RETURN:

Our location-specific employee benefits are designed to cater to the unique needs of Tideans:

25 days paid annual leave

3 paid days off for volunteering or L&D activities

Extended maternity and paternity leave covered by the company

Personal L&D budget

Additional health & dental insurance

Mental wellbeing platform

Fully covered Multisports card

Food vouchers

Snacks, light food, drinks in the office

WFH equipment allowance

Flexible working from home

Sabbatical Leave

Share options

TIDEAN WAYS OF WORKING:

At Tide, we champion a flexible workplace model that supports both in-person and remote work to cater to the specific needs of our different teams.

While remote work is supported, we believe in the power of face-to-face interactions to foster team spirit and collaboration. Our offices are designed as hubs for innovation and team-building, where we encourage regular in-person gatherings to foster a strong sense of community.

#LI-CC1, #LI-Remote

TIDE IS A PLACE FOR EVERYONE

At Tide, we believe that we can only succeed if we let our differences enrich our culture. Our Tideans come from a variety of backgrounds and experience levels. We consider everyone irrespective of their ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, family or parental status, national origin, veteran, neurodiversity or differently-abled status. We celebrate diversity in our workforce as a cornerstone of our success. Our commitment to a broad spectrum of ideas and backgrounds is what enables us to build products that resonate with our members’ diverse needs and lives.

We are One Team and foster a transparent and inclusive environment, where everyone’s voice is heard.

At Tide, we thrive on diversity, embracing various backgrounds and experiences. We welcome all individuals regardless of ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity, or disability. Our inclusive culture is key to our success, helping us build products that meet our members’ diverse needs. We are One Team, committed to transparency and ensuring everyone’s voice is heard.

Disclaimer

It has come to our attention that individuals or agencies are falsely claiming to represent Tide and are reaching out to candidates regarding job opportunities. Please be aware that:

  • Tide does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process.
  • All official Tide job opportunities are listed exclusively on our Careers Page and applications should be submitted through this channel.
  • Communication from Tide will only come from an official @tide .co email address.
  • Tide does not work with agencies or recruiters without prior formal engagement, and we do not authorize third parties to make job offers on our behalf.

If you are contacted by anyone misrepresenting Tide or requesting payment, please treat it as fraudulent and report it to us immediately at talent@tide.co

Your safety and trust are important to us, and we are committed to ensuring a fair and transparent recruitment process.

Tide leverages AI to enhance our hiring experience. You can read more about how we use AI in our recruitment process in our AI Policy.

Your personal data will be processed by Tide for recruitment purposes and in accordance with Tide’s Recruitment Privacy Notice.

jobs.title: Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Hello, we’re UW

We’re a bunch of great people working together, solving problems and having fun. Together, we strive to find solutions to help the great people around us get on with the things that really matter in life.

How? It’s simple, we take the headache out of essential home services by providing them all in one place – energy, broadband, mobile, and even insurance.  That’s one provider, one account, one bill, and lots of savings.

We’re a truly unique FTSE 250 company with a business model that has real social impact at the heart of it. Being a part of the UW community you’ll join a working environment like no other - one where everyone matters - be it our customers, our partners or you, our people.

Job Description

We deliver progress. What you’ll do and how you will make an impact.

You will own the end-to-end digital product lifecycle of our insurance policies, driving growth by expanding our financial services footprint while ensuring a best-in-class customer experience.

Your core responsibilities will include:

  • Strategic Planning: Building and executing 3–6 month quarterly product plans for your squad and owning key product KPIs and OKRs.
  • Journey Optimisation: Continually refining both customer-facing and advisor-facing journeys to ensure seamless, high-quality experiences.
  • Backlog Prioritisation: Managing feature backlogs using frameworks like RICE or MoSCoW, balancing business impact with delivery dependencies.
  • Vendor Management: Collaborating with third-party software providers to ensure technical delivery aligns with UW standards.
  • Market Insight: Monitoring market trends and the competitive landscape to validate solutions through customer research.
  • Regulatory Rigor: Proactively adhering to financial services regulations and Consumer Duty standards

We work together. Your team and the people you will work with…

You will join our Technology & Product function as a key member of a cross-functional squad. You will act as the primary bridge between UX designers, Engineering, Data, and Commercial teams. Your mission is to contribute to the strategy for our core Insurance portfolio—including Home, Motor, and Boiler & Home Cover—ensuring all stakeholders are aligned on priorities and progress.

Qualifications

We put people first. It’s all about you..

To thrive in this role, you should be a customer-obsessed problem solver with a dedication to empathy-led design and delivering fair value. You are a strong communicator who can influence cross-functional partners and present clear product roadmaps to leadership using data-backed business cases.

Your technical toolkit and experience should include:

  • Proven Digital Product Management Experience: A track record of managing complex digital products and the full product lifecycle from discovery to launch.
  • Domain Expertise: Experience within the Insurance sector (Home, Motor, or Home Care) is highly advantageous. Experience in highly regulated environments is also preferred.
  • Technical Literacy: Proficiency in Agile methodologies (Scrum/Kanban) and comfortable participating in technical discussion with developers.
  • Data-Driven Decision Making: Skilled in using product analytics tools like Mixpanel or Google Analytics to justify tactical decisions and prioritise backlogs.
  • Specification Mastery: Ability to write clear, concise technical specifications and user acceptance criteria.
  • Third-Party Integration: Experience navigating the nuances of working with external software vendors and APIs.

Additional Information

So why pick UW?

We’ve got big ambitions so there’s going to be plenty of challenges. There are also a lot of benefits:

  • An industry benchmarked salary. We’ll share it during your first conversation.
  • Share Options and a Save as You Earn scheme.
  • Flexible working — remote-first with access to hot desks across the UK and a central London hub.
  • Work-from-anywhere policy for up to 2 weeks per year.
  • Discounts on our services and a free Cashback Card.
  • Matched-contribution pension scheme and life assurance up to 4x salary.
  • Family-friendly policies designed to help you and your family thrive.
  • Discounted private health insurance, Employee Assistance line and free Virtual GP.
  • Belonging groups helping shape an inclusive future.
  • A commitment to learning and progression through our Career Development Framework with regular review and promotion cycles

Apply here!

You’ve got this far… Hit apply - we can’t wait to hear from you! Worried you don’t hit all the criteria? We welcome applications from diverse and varied backgrounds so get your application in and let’s chat!

Claire Fennell will be your point of contact throughout the recruitment process.

Not sure you meet all the requirements? Let us decide! Research shows that women and members of other underrepresented groups tend not to apply for jobs if they think they may not meet every qualification, when in fact they often do.

We provide equal opportunities, a diverse and inclusive work environment, and fairness for everyone. You are welcome to apply no matter your age, disability, gender, marriage or civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity status, race, religion or belief, or sexual orientation. Please don’t be afraid to ask about what we can do to support your needs. All requests will be carefully and fairly considered.

Please note, if you are successful and offered a role at UW, you will be subject to a background check. Where checks are unsatisfactory or incomplete and/or a failure to reveal information relating to convictions that you are required to identify as part of the background checks, could lead to withdrawal of an offer of employment.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager - Doctor to Patient flow
jobs.jd_full:
We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to join the Practice Efficiency team in Berlin.

You will work on Doctolib’s holistic solution to streamline the care journey by collecting and sharing complete, up-to-date, and structured information required for care delivery. It transforms how health professionals prepare for consultations, how administrative staff manage patient workflows, and how patients experience healthcare, replacing fragmented, paper-based processes with a seamless digital experience. You will collaborate with multiple product teams on a critical product area at the intersection of clinical data and user experience, while having measurable impact on both Patient and Healthcare Professional NPS.

What you will do

Your responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Define and execute the product vision, strategy and roadmap for one or several capabilities of our practice efficiency domain
  • Collaborate with engineers, designers, clinical experts, and go-to-market stakeholders to build solutions that serve multiple user personas across all Doctolib’s markets
  • Conduct user research and competitive analysis to identify unmet needs and drive prioritization grounded in data
  • Ensure regulatory and market-specific compliance while maintaining a scalable, country-agnostic architecture
  • Foster a culture of collaboration, innovation, and continuous learning within the broader product management team, with the potential of leading other product managers

Who you are

Before you read on: if you don’t have the exact profile described below, but you feel this job description matches your skill set, we still encourage you to apply.

You could be our next team mate if you:

  • Have at least 5 years of experience in Product Management having worked with developers, designers, customers, stakeholders and leadership (Technical Product Management is a plus)
  • Base your strategic product decisions on a strong analytical mindset grounded on data and customer needs - solving complex problems
  • Have developed and launched products in a large scale SaaS or B2B environment, where you needed to change the engine while it ran at full speed
  • Always put the user in the center of your work
  • Are fluent in English (German or French is a plus)

Now it would be fantastic if you:

  • Have experience in the healthcare industry and/or leveraging state of the art technology such as AI

What we offer

  • A Deutschlandticket (Germany-wide public transport pass) fully paid for by Doctolib
  • 28 vacation days + 1 additional day for each full calendar year of employment (up to a maximum of 30 days)
  • Work from abroad for up to 10 days per year thanks to our flexibility days policy
  • Company health insurance with great supplementary benefits through our partner Allianz
  • Company pension scheme (bAV) through Allianz with an employer subsidy of 40% (15% within the probationary period)
  • The Doctolib Parent Care program, which includes one month additional parental leave and much more
  • Free mental health and coaching services through our partner Moka.care
  • Subsidized sports membership through our partner Urban Sports Club
  • A flexible workplace policy offering both hybrid and office-based mode
  • Alongside healthy snacks and our regular breakfast buffet, we provide a subsidized meal benefit
  • For caregivers and workers with disabilities, a package including an adaptation of the remote policy, extra days off for medical reasons, and psychological support

The interview process

  • Recruiter Interview
  • Manager Interview
  • Case Study
  • Final Interview
  • At least one reference check

Job details

  • Permanent position
  • Full-Time / Part-Time
  • Workplace: Berlin
  • Start date: As soon as possible
  • Compensation: fix + bonus on objectives (according to your profile)

If you would like to find out more about tech life at Doctolib, feel free to read our latest Medium blog articles!

At Doctolib, we are committed to improving access to healthcare for everyone. This translates into our recruitment process. We evaluate candidates based solely on qualifications and motivation, without any form of discrimination.

The more diverse ideas are heard, the more our product will truly improve healthcare for all. You are welcome to apply to Doctolib, regardless of your gender, religion, age, sexual orientation, ethnicity, disability.

To ensure equal opportunities, we invite you to exclude personal information (e.g. pictures, age) from your applications. If you require any accommodation, please let us know for support during the hiring process.

Join us in building the healthcare we all dream of!

All information provided is processed by Doctolib for application management. For data processing details, click here .

Please contact hr.dataprivacy(AT)doctolib.com to exercise your rights (but not for applications).

jobs.title: Product Manager Digital Marketing Enablement
jobs.jd_full:
IXL Learning, developer of personalized learning products used by millions of people globally, seeks a Product Manager for IXL’s digital marketing platform. In this role, you will design a data pipeline to supply our marketing teams with the data they need to drive multi-channel digital marketing initiatives that power our strategic business goals and operational needs. #LI-CC1

This is a full-time position in our San Mateo, CA headquarters office. The work schedule for this role is Monday-Friday in the office with the option to work from home one day per week.

WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

  • Develop a strategic understanding of IXL’s digital marketing efforts and how they’re critical to our business
  • Collaborate with our digital marketing and email marketing teams to better reach and connect with current and prospective customer
  • Dig into our data - deeply understand what data is available and how it’s organized to enable impactful targeted and differentiated marketing
  • Design simple, elegant, and robust solutions that address business needs and align with broader business strategy; provide detailed written specifications for each solution
  • Help define our approach to address issues and opportunities, focusing on effective integrations with other systems - creating new functionality and enhancing and scaling current functionality
  • Design data stores and pipelines that optimize digital marketing performance and efficiency, and enable rapid experimentation
  • Work with engineering to build and deploy a robust data pipeline that supports digital marketing operations

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in STEM or a related field
  • 5+ years of experience in product management
  • Experience focused on multi-channel digital marketing enablement is preferred, with an emphasis on email marketing
  • Knowledge of databases and SQL
  • Understanding of data pipeline architecture and best practices
  • Strong communication skills
  • Independent and highly motivated, with a resourceful, “figure-it-out” approach
  • Extremely strong analytical skills - able to provide high quality, efficient, creative solutions to complex problems
  • Curiosity, open-mindedness, and an unfailingly positive attitude

The base salary range for this full-time position is $130,000 to $180,000 + equity + benefits. Our pay ranges are determined by role, level, and location. IXL Learning is the country’s largest EdTech company. We reach millions of learners through our diverse range of products. For example:

  • Rosetta Stone provides an immersive learning experience for 25 languages

Our mission is to create innovative products that will make a real, positive difference for learners and educators and we’re looking for passionate, mission-minded people to join us in achieving this goal. We approach our work with passion, tenacity, and authenticity. We find it immensely satisfying to develop products that impact the lives of millions and we are eager to have you join our team.

At IXL, we value diversity in age, race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, physical and mental ability, political and religious beliefs, and life experience, and we are proud to promote a work environment where everyone, from any background, can do their best work. IXL Learning is an equal opportunity employer.

jobs.title: Staff Technical Product Manager Infotainment
jobs.jd_full:
Here at Scout Motors, we’re carrying forward the heritage of one of the most iconic American vehicles in history. A vehicle dating back to 1960. One that forged the path for future generations of rugged SUVs and trucks and will do so once again.

But Scout is more than just a brand, it’s a legacy steeped in a culture of exploration, caretaking, and hard work.

The Scout brand is all about respect. Respect for the past and the future by taking an iconic American brand that hasn’t been around for a while, electrifying it, digitizing it, and loading it with American innovation. Respect for communities by creating a company that stands for its people and its customers. Respect for both work and play, with vehicles that are equally at home at a camp site, a job site, or on a Tuesday commute. And respect for our customers by developing two powertrains that meet their requirements — an all-electric powertrain as well as the Harvester™ range extender powertrain which includes a built-in gas-powered generator with an estimated 500 miles of combined range.

At Scout Motors, we empower our talented, inclusive, and entrepreneurial teams to innovate. What makes a Scout employee? Someone who is a visionary and a leader, who seeks new paths and shares lessons learned. A knowledgeable doer who collaborates across the company to build better. A go-getter with unrivaled passion.

Join us at Scout Motors and be part of shaping the future of transportation. If you’re ready to drive change and make history, apply now!

Overview

We’re looking for a hands-on Technical Program Manager to lead infotainment-first programs in a fast-moving automotive environment. You’ll own end-to-end delivery of infotainment platforms—from head units and HMI to multimedia and backend services—while making sure connectivity features like telematics, OTA updates, and backend integrations work seamlessly.

You’ll coordinate across brands, suppliers, and internal teams to turn complex programs into smooth, on-time deliveries that delight drivers.

What you’ll do

Become part of an iconic brand that is set to revolutionize the electric pick-up truck & rugged SUV marketplace by achieving the following:

  • Lead end-to-end infotainment program delivery, from concept to production and OTA releases
  • Drive infotainment platforms (Android Automotive / embedded Linux, multimedia, HMI) and connectivity features (telematics, OTA, cloud integration)
  • Ensure features work seamlessly across vehicle, backend, and user experience
  • Coordinate with brands, Tier-1 suppliers, and internal teams
  • Manage schedules, dependencies, risks, and integrations
  • Support SOW definition, long-term planning, and operational readiness
  • Provide clear status updates, escalate issues, and make cross-team decisions

Location & Travel Expectations:

  • This role will be based out of the Scout Motors location in Fremont, CA.
  • This role may be based out of the Scout Motors corporate headquarters in Charlotte, NC. This role may be remote to start, but will transition to an in-office setting at the headquarters within 3-6 months of start date. This role is not eligible for remote work in New York City.
  • This role requires 4-5 days per week in the office, with regular in-person meetings and events.
  • Applicants should expect that the role will require the ability to convene with Scout colleagues in person and travel to participate in events on behalf of the company from time to time.

What you’ll bring

We expect all Scout employees to have integrity, curiosity, resourcefulness, and strive to exhibit a positive attitude, as well as a growth mindset. You’ll be comfortable with change and flexible in a fast-paced, high-growth environment. You’ll take a collaborative approach to achieve ambitious goals. Here’s what else you’ll bring:

  • 7+ years in Technical Program Management or Systems Engineering
  • 3+ years focused on automotive infotainment or embedded platforms
  • Deep understanding of infotainment architecture, HMI, multimedia, and connected services
  • Experience delivering programs across multiple brands

jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager App Modernization
jobs.jd_full:
The Application Modernization Platform team aids developers in making the shift from relational databases to MongoDB, building toolkits that leverage AI to power seamless transitions.

As a Senior Technical Program Manager, you will own the successful delivery of complex, cross-functional efforts for Application Modernization. You will partner closely with stakeholders across the organization from Engineering, Product, Product Design, and Field-facing teams to develop and execute against our overall product vision and business objectives, uncover solutions to technical challenges, and provide data-driven insights that power strategic decision-making.

This position is based out of our New York City office, with the possibility of working from another MongoDB office or remotely for the right candidate.

The right candidate for this role will be:

  • Experienced with 5+ years of Software development background, preferably with experience with Java web services and with relational data modelling and SQL
  • Familiar with tools and methodologies for code analysis, refactoring, and automated testing
  • Able to partner with technical stakeholders in methodically breaking down engineering challenges to deliver against abstract goals
  • Able to participate in technical design discussions and summarize trade-offs, outcomes, and next steps
  • Skilled at communicating across a diverse set of engineers and stakeholders
  • Capable of deep user empathy and adept at balancing product goals and engineering constraints to deliver impactful experiences
  • Hyper-organized and capable of coordinating across multiple independent work streams and organizations
  • A role model for effective execution practices, driven by an attuned sense of priority and urgency
  • Able to leverage their experience in program delivery to influence improvements to our tools, operations, architecture
  • Trained in working with project tracking software (JIRA is preferred)
  • Familiar with MongoDB or a comparable technology
  • Exposed to generative AI and Machine Learning
  • Interested in business automation work such as scripting in Python, Google Apps Script and Slack

Position Expectations:

  • Leverage technical acumen and analytical skills to drive engineering programs forward and maximize business value delivery
  • Recognize patterns in a sea of information and take action accordingly
  • Develop a strategic approach to program management that balances hands-on oversight with process-driven operationalization. This involves discerning when to provide direct guidance and when to empower teams by establishing clear frameworks and guidelines for ongoing initiatives
  • Build strategic partnerships with Product, Design, and Engineering stakeholders
  • Expand knowledge into new domains as called upon
  • Embody MongoDB’s core valuesand leadership principles

Success Measures:

  • In 3 months you will be ramped on the App Modernization product and engineering roadmap
  • In 6 months you will own and drive critical initiatives across the App Modernization portfolio
  • In 12 months you will be organizing multiple high impact efforts and integrating improvements to frameworks for managing critical programs at MongoDB

About MongoDB

MongoDB is built for change, empowering our customers and our people to innovate at the speed of the market. We have redefined the database for the AI era, enabling innovators to create, transform, and disrupt industries with software. MongoDB’s unified database platform, the most widely available, globally distributed database on the market, helps organizations modernize legacy workloads, embrace innovation, and unleash AI. Our cloud-native platform, MongoDB Atlas, is the only globally distributed, multi-cloud database and is available across AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure.

With offices worldwide and over 60,000 customers, including 75% of the Fortune 100 and AI-native startups, relying on MongoDB for their most important applications, we’re powering the next era of software.

Our compass at MongoDB is our Leadership Commitment, guiding how and why we make decisions, show up for each other, and win. It’s what makes us MongoDB.

To drive the personal growth and business impact of our employees, we’re committed to developing a supportive and enriching culture for everyone. From employee affinity groups, to fertility assistance and a generous parental leave policy, we value our employees’ wellbeing and want to support them along every step of their professional and personal journeys. Learn more about what it’s like to work at MongoDB, and help us make an impact on the world!

MongoDB is committed to providing any necessary accommodations for individuals with disabilities within our application and interview process. To request an accommodation due to a disability, please inform your recruiter.

MongoDB, Inc. provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type and makes all hiring decisions without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

Compensation at the time of offer is unique to each candidate and based on a variety of factors such as skill set, experience, qualifications, and work location. Salary is one part of MongoDB’s total compensation and benefits package. Other benefits for eligible employees may include: equity, participation in the employee stock purchase program, flexible paid time off, 20 weeks fully-paid gender-neutral parental leave, fertility and adoption assistance, 401(k) plan, mental health counseling, access to transgender-inclusive health insurance coverage, and health benefits offerings. jobs.title: IT Senior Project Manager Finance
jobs.jd_full:
Your Impact

We are looking for an IT Senior Project Manager Finance to join the Core Finance Squad team in IT.

Your mission will be to lead end-to-end delivery of complex IT projects that evolve our financial information systems across France, Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands. You will implement best-in-class solutions and tools to support our teams in serving the healthcare professionals who use Doctolib every day, as well as the millions of Europeans who visit our sites every month.

Working in the tech team at Doctolib means building innovative products and features to improve the daily lives of care teams and patients.

What you’ll build

Your responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Lead end-to-end delivery of complex Finance IT projects, serving as the main point of contact for Finance teams (Accounting, FP&A, Procurement, Treasury, Consolidation, Internal Control) and ensuring delivery within scope, budget, and timeline

  • Collect and formalize business requirements, prioritize initiatives, and facilitate steering committees to ensure consistent communication to all stakeholders and sponsors across multiple simultaneous projects

  • Build and coordinate the Core Finance IT roadmap, managing project identification, prioritization, resource planning, and budget commitments while leveraging Doctolib’s project management framework

  • Manage projects covering process transformation and tool implementation (ERP configuration, system integrations, automations), performing Discovery and configuration on new modules while coaching the engineering team

  • Structure support and training on Finance tools (SAP S/4HANA, Concur, Egencia, SAP Integration Suite, Pivot), ensuring best practices are documented and reference materials are built for configuration decisions

  • Drive change management and user adoption for new tools and processes, building trusted relationships with Finance stakeholders and translating business needs into actionable IT deliverables while managing trade-offs transparently

What you’ll bring

Before you read on: if you don’t have the exact profile described below, but you feel this job description matches your skill set, we still encourage you to apply.

You’ll be a great fit if you:

  • You have 5+ years of experience in IT project management, AMOA, or functional consulting in a Finance systems environment with proven track record on ERP projects covering P2P, AP, AR, GL, Assets, and Expenses

  • You have strong command of project management methodologies including scoping, planning, RACI, steering committees, and budget tracking, with ability to run design workshops and translate business requirements into functional specifications

  • You own your projects end-to-end with accountability from first workshop to post-go-live hypercare, proactively identifying risks, proposing solutions, and never losing sight of user experience

  • You speak both business and IT, understanding Finance processes well enough to challenge requirements and communicate clearly with Finance VPs, accountants, developers, and architects alike

  • You are rigorous and structured, ensuring your projects have clear charters, documented decisions, tested solutions, and trained users, recognizing that adoption is key to success

  • You manage complexity with pragmatism, juggling multiple projects and shifting priorities while making transparent trade-offs and keeping stakeholders aligned across international teams

  • You are fluent in French and English (business-level English required)

It would be fantastic if you:

  • You have experience working in a fast-paced, international scale-up or large corporate transformation context

  • You have a background in IS Finance roles or consulting firms (Big 4, functional consulting)

  • You have familiarity with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Egencia, Concur, and integration middleware (SAP Integration Suite)

  • You have knowledge of consolidation tools (e.g., SAP Group reporting, Anaplan, Viareport) or treasury tools (e.g., Kyriba, Diapason, FIS)

Life at Doctolib Tech

  • Our solutions are built on a single fully cloud-native platform that supports web and mobile app interfaces, multiple languages, and is adapted to country and healthcare specialty requirements.

  • Our stack is composed of Rails, TypeScript, Java, Python, Kotlin, Swift, and React Native.

  • We leverage AI ethically across our products to empower patients and health professionals. Discover our AI vision here.

Want to learn more about our tech culture and environment? Visit the Doctolib Tech site .

What we offer

  • Free comprehensive health insurance for you and your children

  • 25 days of paid vacation per year, plus up to 14 days of RTT

  • Free mental health and coaching services through our partner Moka.care

  • Work from abroad for up to 10 days per year thanks to our flexibility days policy

  • Lunch vouchers (Swile card) worth €8.50 per working day, with €4.50 covered by Doctolib

  • A subsidy from the work council to refund part of the membership to a sport club or a creative class

  • 50% reimbursement of your public transport subscription

  • Parent Care Program: receive one additional month of leave on top of the legal parental leave

  • For caregivers and workers with disabilities, a package including an adaptation of the remote policy, extra days off for medical reasons, and psychological support

  • Relocation support in case of international mobility

  • Access to the best AI tools for coding, development and dedicated training

jobs.title: Product Manager District Integrations
jobs.jd_full:
IXL Learning, developer of personalized learning products used by millions of people globally, is seeking a Product Manager for our District Integrations team, which has a vital role in helping schools and districts successfully use IXL in their digital learning ecosystems. In this role, you will help shape the vision and oversee execution of integrations that connect IXL to customer platforms like Learning Management Systems (LMS), data warehouses, rostering solutions and more. Your work will help ensure that IXL fits seamlessly into classrooms across the country, including many of the biggest school districts in the US. #LI-TB1

ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Product Manager for District Integrations, you will help connect customer needs with technical development while working cross functionally. A critical part of this position involves engaging directly with customers. You’ll attend key sales calls and strategic customer meetings to understand education leaders’ needs and challenges, collect feedback and identify opportunities. You’ll translate those insights into clear product ideas and enhancements that shape the future of our integrations roadmap. You’ll also partner closely with engineering to define and build high-impact, elegant solutions, and you’ll engage with internal teams to ensure they understand how our integrations meet the needs of educators and administrators. Those teams can include sales, account management, customer support, and more.

This is a full-time position in our San Mateo, CA headquarters office. The work schedule for this role is Monday-Friday in the office with the option to work from home one day per week.

WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

  • Develop a keen understanding of evolving district needs through participation in key sales and customer meetings, cutting through the noise to hone in on critical needs
  • Identify and articulate solutions that marry IXL product vision and key district needs
  • Collaborate with key stakeholders and company leaders to define the vision, strategy, and roadmap for district integrations
  • Think through detailed product requirements and maintain clear documentation; collaborating with engineers, designers, and other PMs as appropriate
  • Partner with your engineering lead(s) to maximize the success of your dedicated team in your product area; this critical partnership includes everything from managing projects and sprints, third-party vendor management and troubleshooting, evaluating development resource allocation, internal communications and training, and more
  • Effectively communicate priorities and status to a wider business audience, provide suggested customer messaging, escalate concerns, and closely monitor post-launch success and progress

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

  • Bachelor’s degree in STEM or a related field
  • 5+ years of product experience, with proven success delivering highly technical product offerings from conception through launch and beyond; experience with B2B SaaS integrations strongly preferred
  • Exceptionally strong listener, strong relationship-builder (both internally and externally), strong leadership and clear communication
  • Strong analytical skills - ability to provide high quality, efficient, creative solutions to complex technical problems
  • Ability to think critically, operate with incomplete information, and explain your decisions clearly
  • Highly motivated with a resourceful, “figure-it-out” approach
  • Ability to work effectively with diverse cross-functional teams that include engineering, sales, customer support, marketing, design, curriculum, and product and company leadership
  • Familiarity with edtech is a plus

Our pay ranges are determined by role, level, and location. The base salary range for this full-time position is $130,000 to $180,000 + equity + benefits. The range displayed on each job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire pay for the position. Individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

ABOUT IXL LEARNING

IXL Learning is the country’s largest EdTech company. We reach millions of learners through our diverse range of products. For example:

  • 1 in 4 students in the United States uses IXL.com
  • Rosetta Stone provides an immersive learning experience for 25 languages
  • Wyzant is the nation’s largest community of tutors, covering 300+ subjects
  • Teachers Pay Teachers (TPT) is a comprehensive marketplace for millions of educator-created resources

Our mission is to create innovative products that will make a real, positive difference for learners and educators and we’re looking for passionate, mission-minded people to join us in achieving this goal. We have a unique culture at IXL that fosters collaboration and the open exchange of ideas. We value our team and treat one another with kindness and respect. We approach our work with passion, tenacity, and authenticity. We find it immensely satisfying to develop products that impact the lives of millions and we are eager to have you join our team.

At IXL, we value diversity in age, race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, physical and mental ability, political and religious beliefs, and life experience, and we are proud to promote a work environment where everyone, from any background, can do their best work. IXL Learning is an equal opportunity employer.

jobs.title: GTM Strategy and Operations Analyst
jobs.jd_full:
GTM Strategy & Operations, Data Analyst

We’re looking for a Data Analyst I to join our growing GTM Strategy & Operations team. Our team tackles complex, high-impact problems that require insight, creativity, and precision to build scalable processes, tools, and systems for a rapidly expanding global business.

In this role, you’ll help architect, enrich, and optimize the data that powers our go-to-market strategy. You’ll work across systems and teams to design and maintain scalable data pipelines, ensure the accuracy and reliability of core datasets, and enable actionable insights for the Sales and Marketing organizations. Your work will directly influence how the organization uses GTM data to drive strategy, measure performance, and accelerate growth.

At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.

What You’ll Do:

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines and workflows that power the GTM organization
  • Own the operational data layer that supports core GTM metrics, dashboards, and automation workflows
  • Develop robust data models in dbt (or similar tools) to transform raw data into clean, reliable datasets used across teams
  • Partner with internal stakeholders to define and implement business logic that improves data quality, accuracy, and consistency across systems
  • Manage and optimize ETL processes to integrate data from multiple internal and external sources into centralized repositories
  • Build repeatable frameworks for data validation, monitoring, and enrichment to ensure reliable insights
  • Collaborate closely with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success to identify data needs, define metrics, and enable self-service analytics
  • Work cross-functionally to improve how GTM data flows between Salesforce, Snowflake, and visualization tools like Metabase, Tableau, or Power BI
  • Contribute flexibly to high-priority projects that require rapid data-driven problem-solving in a fast-paced environment

Who You Are:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or equivalent
  • Minimum two years of relevant professional experience, including at least one year supporting sales teams or working with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce)
  • Proficiency in SQL, with the ability to write and optimize complex queries independently
  • Experience with ETL tools such as dbt or similar frameworks for transforming and modeling data
  • Comfort working in spreadsheets like Google Sheets or Excel for data analysis and reporting
  • Experience with modern data warehouses such as Snowflake, Redshift, or BigQuery
  • Familiarity with Python, including an understanding of core data concepts, scripting fundamentals, and automation
  • Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly present data-driven insights to both technical and non-technical audiences
  • Experience using visualization tools such as Metabase, Tableau, or Power BI
  • A naturally curious, analytical mindset with a drive to uncover the “why” behind the data

Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That’s okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.

Benefits and Growth:

  • Continuous training and career growth
  • New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan
  • Generous and competitive US benefits
  • Friendly and inclusive workplace culture

Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.

About Datadog:

Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on Instagram, LinkedIn, and Datadog Learning Center.

Equal Opportunity at Datadog:

Datadog is proud to offer equal employment opportunity to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our Candidate Legal Notices for your reference.

If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete this form. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.
